title: ERC1271 isValidSignature with nested EIP-712 (ERC7739) and short-circuit detection
explanation: Two key optimizations: (1) ERC7739 auto-detection — if signature is empty and hash equals the magic 0x7739...7739 sentinel, return 0x77390001 to signal nested EIP-712 support without a separate function. ~0 / 0xffff * 0x7739 produces the pattern in compiler-friendly bytecode. (2) Branchless return value: shl(224, or(0x1626ba7e, sub(0, iszero(success)))) returns 0x1626ba7e on success and 0xffffffff on failure. sub(0, iszero(success)) is 0 when success=true and ~0 (all bits set) when success=false; or-ing with the magic preserves it when bits are 0 and saturates to 0xffffffff otherwise. shl(224) places the bytes4 in the canonical right-padded position for return.
when_to_apply: Any ERC1271-compliant smart account that wants to advertise ERC7739 support and avoid branching on the success path
when_not_to_apply: Accounts that need to return non-standard error codes or perform additional logging on signature failure
